changes_applied.fix: Rewrite description with prerequisites + invocation phrases
changes_applied.details: New description makes clear this runs AFTER a plan exists, explains the 3-strike fix loop concisely, and names the attempt-directory isolation pattern. Added 6 new YAML triggers ('run validation', 'execute validation', 'run the plan', 'fix and retry', etc.). Expanded from 27 to ~80 words.

changes_applied.fix: Add 'When to use' section clarifying plan-first sequencing
changes_applied.details: New section explicitly states forge-execute requires a plan from forge-plan or create-validation-plan. Explains the rationale: without PASS criteria the fix loop has nothing to converge on. Prevents users from invoking forge-execute blind.

changes_applied.fix: Expand Modes table with when-to-use + fix-loop behavior
changes_applied.details: Old table had 3 columns: Mode, Use Case, Behavior. New table clarifies when each mode is right (pre-release, pre-commit smoke, CI, debug/partial fix) and explicitly calls out that CI mode DISABLES the fix loop — critical safety detail since auto-fixes in CI can mutate the branch under test.

changes_applied.fix: Expand Phase 3 (Analyze Failures) with ordered classification
changes_applied.details: Old Phase 3 was 3 terse bullets. New version is a 4-step ordered protocol: (1) read actual evidence — don't guess from verdict; (2) compare to criteria — which one failed, actual vs expected; (3) classify into code bug / config / environment / flaky with per-category implications; (4) decide fixable vs BLOCKED with criteria for each. Includes concrete example at end.

Old rebuild rule was one sentence. New version is 3 bullets covering the always-rebuild rule, what happens if rebuild fails (that's the new failure; strike increments; fix the compile error), and how to handle skipped rebuilds (null strike, redo).

changes_skipped.fix: Inject state schema excerpt into Phase 0 or Phase 5
changes_skipped.reason: The forge-state-schema.md reference is already linked in two places. Inlining an excerpt would duplicate content and the schema is 200+ lines — hurts readability more than it helps. Keep the reference link.

The forge loop is inherently stateful (reads plan, writes state, rebuilds, re-executes) — a naive shell orchestrator would be error-prone. Better handled by subagents with full tool access when usage permits.

description_before: Execute validation journeys with autonomous fix loop: run, capture evidence, analyze, rebuild, re-execute (max 3 strikes). Use after plan exists; maintains attempt history in isolated directories.
description_after: Use after a validation plan exists to actually run the journeys and, when they fail, autonomously try to fix them. Runs each journey against the real system, captures evidence into a fresh attempt directory, analyzes failures, applies a fix, rebuilds, and re-runs — up to 3 strikes before giving up on that journey. Preserves the full attempt history (e2e-evidence/forge-attempt-N/) so you can see exactly what changed between tries. Reach for it on phrases like 'run validation', 'execute the plan', 'validate now', 'fix the failures and retry', or after forge-plan produced a plan.
self_review_notes: The CI-mode fix-loop-disabled clarification is the most important change — auto-fixes in CI can mutate the branch under test and produce corrupt evidence. The expanded Phase 3 gives the model a real diagnostic protocol instead of 'classify the bug' hand-waving, which is where the fix loop actually converges or diverges.
